name: 4. COCO YOLO Convolutional Neural Network (Keras)
description: A convolutional YOLO-style object detection network built from scratch with TensorFlow/Keras, trained on the MS COCO 2017 dataset. The model predicts bounding boxes and class probabilities on a 13×13 grid using a custom YOLO loss function. Despite a healthy loss curve, the model failed to learn spatial detection and defaulted to predicting 'person' in the centre of every image due to class imbalance in COCO.
architecture: 416×416×3 → Conv(64,3×3)+BN+ReLU → MaxPool → Conv(128,3×3)+BN+ReLU → MaxPool → Conv(256,3×3)+BN+ReLU → MaxPool → Conv(256,3×3)+BN+ReLU+Dropout(0.3) → MaxPool → Conv(512,3×3)+BN+ReLU+Dropout(0.3) → MaxPool → Conv(170,1×1,sigmoid) → 13×13×170
techniques: YOLO grid-based object detection (S=13, B=2, C=80)
Custom YOLO loss with 5 weighted components (coord, size, objectness, no-object, class)
Batch Normalisation after every Conv layer

results.summary: Training loss decreased from 0.0759 to 0.0369 over 24 epochs and val_loss tracked closely (final 0.0606), suggesting no overfitting. However the model failed to learn meaningful object detection — it predicted 'person' in the centre for nearly every image regardless of content. Root cause: class imbalance in COCO (~60% of images contain a person) meant the model found a shortcut that minimised loss without learning spatial features. No saved model — the weights were not useful.
